In choosing the great officers of Solomon’s court, his wisdom was certainly evident. Several were the same as those who served in his father's time. A plan was established so that no part of the country would be depleted to supply his court, though each region sent its share.

Never did the crown of Israel shine so brightly as when Solomon wore it. He had peace on all sides. In this way, his kingdom served as a representation of the Messiah’s; for it is promised to Him that he will have the Gentiles as His inheritance, and that rulers will worship Him. The spiritual peace, joy, and holy security of all who are faithful followers of the Lord Jesus were represented by that of Israel. The Kingdom of God is not, as Solomon's was, merely food and drink, but something infinitely better: righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit. The large number of his attendants and the great number of visitors he received are shown by the daily provisions made. In this way, Christ surpasses Solomon, because He feeds all His followers, not with bread that spoils, but with food that gives eternal life.

Solomon’s wisdom was more a source of glory to him than his wealth. He possessed what is called here largeness of heart, for the heart often represents the powers of the mind. He had the gift of speech as well as wisdom. It is very desirable that those who have great gifts should have generous hearts to use them for the benefit of others. What treasures of wisdom and knowledge are lost! However, every kind of knowledge needed for salvation can be found in the holy Scriptures. People came from all parts, more eager for knowledge than their neighbors, to hear the wisdom of Solomon. Solomon was a representation of Christ, in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge; and they are hidden for us, because He has become wisdom from God for us. Christ’s fame will spread throughout the earth, and people from all nations will come to Him, learn from Him, take upon themselves His easy yoke, and find rest for their souls.
